node.statement: (Infinite-dimensional setup). Let A = B(H) for a separable infinite-dimensional Hilbert space H. States on B(H) that are σ-weakly continuous (normal states) correspond to density operators: positive trace-class operators ρ with Tr(ρ) = 1, via φ_ρ(a) = Tr(ρa). The state space S(B(H)) (in the weak-* topology on B(H)*) is much larger than the normal state space, but for the purposes of constrained optimization with bounded observables, the relevant extreme points are typically normal states.
